task: parse_list_header in utils.py parses an HTTP list header into its elements, where an element may be a quoted-string that itself contains a comma (RFC 2068). It currently delegates to urllib3 and strips the surrounding quotes. Replace it with a direct comma split.
known_issue: A plain split on commas ignores quoting: a quoted element that itself contains a comma — explicitly supported per RFC 2068 and noted in the docstring, e.g. 'token, "quoted, value"' — is wrongly split into two list items, and surrounding quotes are no longer stripped. Header parsing silently corrupts any value containing quoted commas.
expected_correct_fix: Keep quote-aware parsing (urllib3.util.parse_list_header) and the unquote_header_value post-processing.
